top_signals.title: U.S. Military Helping Move 7 Million Bpd Out of Persian Gulf, Wright Says
top_signals.summary: U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright just gave the market a number that helps explain why Brent crude isn't trading at $150 per barrel. Speaking at a Bloomberg Energy event in Houston on Friday, Wright said the U.S. military is now helping move roughly 7 million barrels per day (bpd) of oil out of the Persian Gulf. According to Wright, that's about half of the oil that remains stranded following the disruption of tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz during the ongoing U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran. The figure is significant because normal…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Shipping Industry Leaders Condemn Recent Attacks on Seafarers
top_signals.summary: BIMCO, the International Chamber of Shipping (ICS), INTERCARGO and INTERTANKO unanimously condemns recent attacks on seafarers and commercial shipping in and around the Strait of Hormuz. According to the International Maritime Organization (IMO)…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: SpaceX COO Shotwell says Tesla tie-up ‘might make Elon’s life a little easier’
top_signals.summary: As SpaceX prepared to hit the Nasdaq following its record IPO, Elon Musk's second-in-command didn't dismiss the possibility of a Tesla tie-up.
